Set up a multi-node cluster environment with 2 nodes, each having 2 Tesla V100 GPUs (16GB memory) and CUDA 11.0 installed
Install TensorFlow 2.11 GPU version and ensure Python 3.9.7 environment is active
Clone the TensorFlow Models repository at release branch or latest commit matching TensorFlow Model Garden used in the bug report
Export PYTHONPATH to include the root models directory, e.g., /home/cdacapp1/scaling/models-master
Prepare ImageNet dataset in TFRecord format stored in /scratch/cdacapp1/sowmya/actual_imagenet/tf_records with standard ImageNet preprocessing
Create model checkpoint directory at /home/cdacapp1/scaling/models-master/official/legacy/image_classification/checkpointss ensuring write permissions

Prepare two YAML config files for multi-worker distributed training with multi_worker_mirrored strategy: gpu.yaml for task_index 0 and gpu1.yaml for task_index 1, both specifying 2 GPUs and worker_hosts as 'ip1:6655,ip2:6655'

On node 1 (gpu1), run the following commands sequentially: change directory to official/legacy/image_classification, set environment variables PYTHONPATH, DATA_DIR, MODEL_DIR, activate conda environment tf11, and execute python3 classifier_trainer.py with flags --mode=train_and_eval --model_type=resnet --dataset=imagenet --model_dir=$MODEL_DIR --data_dir=$DATA_DIR --config_file=configs/examples/resnet/imagenet/gpu.yaml
On node 2 (gpu2), repeat the above commands but use --config_file=configs/examples/resnet/imagenet/gpu1.yaml and set task_index to 1 in the config

Verify that both nodes successfully connect and initialize the multi_worker_mirrored strategy by inspecting logs for lines indicating detected devices, cluster configuration, and number of replicas in sync (should be 4 devices total)
Measure training throughput (examples/sec) on single GPU to establish baseline (~270 examples/sec)
Measure training throughput on single node with 2 GPUs using MirroredStrategy (not multi_worker) to confirm intra-node scaling (~700 examples/sec)
Measure training throughput on multi-node multi-GPU setup as above, expecting increased throughput but observe same throughput as single GPU (~270 examples/sec), indicating no scaling across nodes
Log and assert that the throughput from multi-node run is approximately equal to the single-GPU throughput within a 10% margin, confirming that distributed training is not scaling
Check training logs for messages about cluster configuration from distribute_utils.configure_cluster confirming worker_hosts and task_index are correctly set on each node
Validate that the global batch size is correctly computed as num_gpus * replicas_in_sync * per_device_batch_size and log it at start of training
Assert that all GPUs are visible on each node by running 'nvidia-smi' during training and confirm GPU utilization is present but throughput does not increase across nodes
Ensure that the datasets are sharded correctly per worker by verifying dataset_builder.global_batch_size and per-replica batch sizes
Confirm that no errors or warnings about cluster connection or device placement appear in logs
Validate that the training loop executes normally but throughput does not scale, confirming the reproduction of the reported bug
